name: eval-1-reusable-prompt-from-description
query: I keep asking Claude to summarize my client meeting notes but the format is different every time. Write me a prompt I can reuse.
expected_behavior: Activates prompt-architect (auto-detect: user says 'Claude keeps getting this wrong')

Phase 1: Asks 4-5 focused questions, one per turn — what the prompt does (input/output), what good output looks like (or asks for an example), what bad output looks like, one-time vs reusable, what variables change each time
Phase 2: Builds the prompt with appropriate structural elements (role, context, task, output format, examples, constraints) — does NOT use every element if the task doesn't need it
Generated prompt has clear placeholder variables for the parts that change each time (e.g., {{meeting_notes}}, {{client_name}})
Generated prompt includes at least one good/bad output example so Claude has format anchors
Output format section is specific (e.g., 'Markdown with these exact headers: Decisions / Action Items (with owner) / Blockers') not vague
Surfaces the prompt as a code block the user can copy
Asks if the user wants to test it on a sample input or save it permanently (chain to prompt-to-skill)
failure_modes_to_check: Skips Phase 1 discovery and produces a generic 'summarize meeting notes' prompt
Generated prompt has no variables, so it can only be used for one specific input
Output format is vague ('produce a clean summary') instead of specific structural requirements
Stacks all 5 discovery questions in one turn
Does not offer the chain to prompt-to-skill for permanent installation
